mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off repricing that depresses equity valuations within a single trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate crossing the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of consumer credit expansion and housing starts captures aggregate demand momentum. When both series accelerate simultaneously, forward equity returns rise due to confirmed earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of RSAFS and HOUST shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX returns over a 5-year out-of-sample test (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding trend indicates robust capacity utilization and pricing power, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO growth rates above trend, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth provides a baseline trend signal for corporate earnings. Sustained positive GDP growth consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy prematurely, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when inflation crosses the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between nonfarm payroll growth and the unemployment rate captures labor market friction. When payroll growth slows while unemployment remains sticky, profit margin compression drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The spread between PAYEMS and UNRATE trends shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.01 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts serve as a leading indicator for consumer confidence and durable goods demand. A sustained increase in housing permits triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as wealth effects boost equity risk appetite.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive skew when HOUST exceeds the monthly growth threshold, or the signal's hit rate drops below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
